institutional_history.description: Sacking of DPM Anwar Ibrahim by PM Mahathir; subsequent prosecution and street mobilisation under the 'Reformasi' banner crystallise a new cleavage axis distinct from the ethnic-religious axes that dominated 1957-1998 politics.
Bersih 1 and 2 rallies (electoral-reform mobilisation), HINDRAF 2007. GE12 (2008) breaks BN's two-thirds majority; the reform-vs-status-quo axis enters electoral salience proper.
GE14: Pakatan Harapan defeats BN. First federal alternation. Reform agenda partially implemented (1MDB prosecutions, IPCMC drafted) then partly reversed.

Reform agenda stalls under Sheraton-era PN government. Sedition Act repeal pledged 2018+2023, withdrawn. Political-financing reform draft (2022) never tabled. The reform axis becomes a dimension of intra-coalition tension within PH itself (T4A issue 1944).

primary_sources.title: Weiss, M. The Roots of Resilience (Cornell, 2020)
Bersih 2.0 (Coalition for Clean and Fair Elections) Annual Reports 2010-2024
Comprehensive coverage in Malaysiakini and The Edge, 1998-2024

notes: The reform/status-quo axis is the cross-cutting cleavage that has the highest variance over time. Unlike ethnic and religious cleavages whose values are stable, individual actors move along this axis (e.g., Mahathir's trajectory from status-quo enforcer 1981-2003 to reform-coalition figurehead 2018-2020 and back to ambiguous post-2020). The axis is ordinal because positions are differentially intense rather than categorical.
